sourceTitle: Build a team wiki in Notion

questions.question: What is the main purpose of creating a team wiki in Notion?

options.text: To store personal notes for each team member
options.explanation: Personal notes are not the focus; the wiki is meant for shared, team‑wide information.

options.text: To serve as a central repository for all important team information
options.explanation: The guide states the wiki acts as a central place for key team info.

options.text: To replace all email communication within the team
options.explanation: While a wiki can reduce some email, it is not intended to replace all email communication.

options.text: To host live video conferences directly on the page
options.explanation: Live video conferencing is not mentioned as a purpose of a team wiki.

questions.question: Which feature helps prevent accidental edits by other team members?

options.explanation: The guide explicitly mentions locking the page to stop accidental edits.
A table of contents organizes information but does not control editing permissions.
Embedding files adds content but does not restrict editing.
A cover photo is purely visual and does not affect edit rights.

questions.question: Which type of content is NOT listed as something you can add inside a team wiki page?

options.explanation: Callouts are mentioned as a content option.
A table of contents is explicitly listed.
The guide does not mention embedding videos; it mentions embedding files from other tools.
Embedding files from other tools is part of the suggested content.

questions.question: How can you quickly keep the wiki up‑to‑date with contributions from teammates?

options.text: By locking the page after each edit
options.explanation: Locking prevents edits, not encourages them.

options.text: By mentioning team members in the page
options.explanation: Mentioning team members notifies them, encouraging timely updates.

options.text: By changing the cover photo weekly
options.explanation: Cover photos are visual; they don’t affect content updates.

options.text: By exporting the wiki as a PDF
options.explanation: Exporting creates a static copy, not a method for keeping the live wiki current.

questions.question: According to the guide, what is the recommended first step when building a team wiki?

options.explanation: The guide’s “Start with a template” section suggests this as the first action.
Inviting external consultants is not mentioned as a first step.

options.text: Create a new workspace for the wiki
options.explanation: While you create a new page, a new workspace is not specified as the initial step.

options.text: Upload all existing documents as PDFs
options.explanation: Uploading PDFs is a possible later action, not the recommended starting point.

questions.question: Approximately how many templates are available in Notion’s template gallery?

options.explanation: The guide mentions far more than 500.
The article explicitly states there are over 10,000 templates.
The number given is an approximation, not an exact figure.
The guide indicates the opposite—there are many more than 1,000.

questions.question: Which action does NOT contribute to customizing the visual branding of your team wiki?

options.explanation: A cover photo is a visual branding element.
A company logo directly reinforces branding.
The guide does not mention changing the page background color as a customization option.

options.text: Adding dynamic links to other pages
options.explanation: Dynamic links improve navigation but are not a visual branding feature.

questions.question: If you need a link that automatically updates when the target page’s title changes, which feature should you use?

options.explanation: Static URLs do not reflect title changes.
Dynamic links adjust automatically to title changes, as described in the guide.

options.text: An embedded PDF of the target page
options.explanation: Embedding a PDF creates a snapshot, not a live link.

options.text: A markdown code block with the page name
options.explanation: Markdown code blocks display text; they don’t create auto‑updating links.
